page: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The CPU includes 32 MB of L3 cache and supports hardware capabilities such as AVX, AES, SHA_NI, and SVM virtualization.

In benchmark evaluations, the b3-8 demonstrates strong single-core performance, scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests and Geekbench single-core metrics. Single-threaded text processing and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top tier. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance metrics, including Geekbench multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, fall into the lower 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large blocks but weak throughput for small cached blocks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable performance without the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances, making it a stable entry-level option for resource-constrained workloads. However, the instance exhibits weak performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput, indicating it is less suited for high-concurrency or I/O-heavy production environments.

This instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded processing tasks, basic software development, and low-traffic web serving where dedicated CPU resources are required at a low resource density.
description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 2 dedicated x86_64 physical cores running at 2.3 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers strong single-core performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng and Geekbench single-core benchmarks, alongside strong single-threaded text processing capabilities. However, its multi-core scaling, memory bandwidth for small blocks, and database operations fall into the lower performance tiers. With dedicated CPU allocation, the b3-8 offers predictable performance for entry-level workloads without burstable CPU credit constraints. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, single-threaded processing tasks, and basic development environments.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 8 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server with NVMe storage, delivering strong single-threaded performance for lightweight general-purpose workloads.

8.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench benchmarks
Predictable dedicated CPU allocation without burstable credit limits
